reason: Moses responds to the Israelites' distress and fear with leadership action. When the people cry out in fear upon seeing the Egyptian army approaching, Moses addresses them directly and takes charge of the situation. He reassures them (אַל־ תִּירָאוּ - 'Fear not') and calls them to stand firm. More significantly, Moses executes God's command to part the sea, serving as the instrumental leader through whom the salvation of the people is accomplished. He stretches out his hand on the sea, directly enabling the Israelites to pass through to safety while the pursuing Egyptians are destroyed. While Moses acknowledges God's ultimate power (הִֽתְיַצְב֗וּ וּרְאוּ֙ אֶת־ יְשׁוּעַ֣ת יְהוָ֔ה - 'stand firm and see the LORD's salvation'), he nevertheless actively leads his people through this life-threatening crisis, which constitutes taking responsibility for their welfare.
